facility: University of Maryland Medical Center — Radiology
indication: Further characterization of the liver lesion seen on MRI; assess enhancement kinetics.
technique: Triple-phase (arterial, portal-venous, delayed) contrast-enhanced CT of the liver.
findings: Large heterogeneously enhancing right-hepatic-lobe lesion with additional left-lobe foci, corresponding to the MRI findings. The enhancement pattern is NON-DIAGNOSTIC for a specific tumor type and does not cleanly fit a benign entity. Right portal-vein thrombus again noted. No definite extrahepatic metastatic disease within the imaged field.
impression: Non-diagnostic but CONCERNING for malignancy. The imaging cannot establish a specific diagnosis — tissue sampling (biopsy) remains the recommended next step for definitive characterization.
